title: QUIC CRT client-side ClientHello PSK-attempt work item
completion_notes: This is the landed client-side PSK-attempt slice. The managed client/runtime path now consumes the dormant detached carrier to emit a PSK-capable ClientHello with binder material while keeping early data closed, but it does not claim resumed-handshake success or any public early-data promise.
out_of_scope: broad support for arbitrary post-handshake TLS messages
fake replay or fake resumption success paths
opportunistic refactors unrelated to the ClientHello PSK-attempt seam
summary: Consume the dormant detached resumption carrier to emit a PSK-capable ClientHello attempt on the managed client/runtime path while keeping early data closed and leaving resumed-handshake success out of scope.
verification_plan: Run focused requirement-home tests for PSK-capable ClientHello attempt construction from dormant detached material, binder proof, early-data closure, bootstrap fallback without dormant material, server-role non-participation, the public no-PSK/no-resumption/no-early-data guard, and unchanged non-resumption handshake/bootstrap behavior; run the current CRT/API/INT guards and the full `REQ_QUIC_CRT_` sweep; run repo-local SpecTrace validation and render checks after the canonical JSON updates.
planned_changes:
- The runtime bootstrap path consumes the dormant detached resumption carrier when it is eligible and keeps the existing bootstrap ClientHello when it is not.
- The key schedule constructs a PSK-capable ClientHello with `psk_key_exchange_modes`, `pre_shared_key`, obfuscated ticket age, and binder bytes derived from the dormant carrier.
- The managed transcript parser accepts the emitted PSK-attempt ClientHello without becoming a general post-handshake TLS engine.
- Focused requirement-home tests cover PSK-attempt ClientHello emission, binder proof, early-data closure, bootstrap fallback, server-role non-participation, and the public no-PSK/no-resumption/no-early-data guard.
- Resumed-handshake success, 0-RTT packet processing, transfer, retry, key update, and any public API widening remain out of scope.
